components.name: Warehouse_shelf.cs
components.description: Representation of the data of each shelf in the warehouse and of the orders.

components.name: Warehouse_node.cs
components.description: Information of each node, its edges and its direction of circulation allowed in X and Z axis.

components.name: NodesRow.cs
components.description: Edges of nodes information, to automatically generate allowed connections between the warehouse nodes without need to specify them manually.

components.name: Warehouse.cs
components.description: Initialization of the robots and the nodes of the warehouse.

components.name: Warehouse_orders.cs
components.description: Assignment of tasks to each robot in a coordinated way.

components.name: OptimalRoute.cs
components.description: Backtracking algorithm for calculating the optimal path between nodes.

components.name: CameraControl.cs
components.description: Camera movement to allow the simulation viewer to move around the warehouse comfortably.

components.name: Warehouse_training.cs
components.description: Automatic training to modify the weights of the robot assignment formula to the optimal values that reduce total processing time between all robots.

components.name: Robot_wheels.cs
components.description: Control of wheels, servo-motors and its speed.

components.name: Robot.cs
components.description: Robot control with kinematics functions, trajectory following systems, collision avoidance strategy, variables about the state of the robot and picking up and unloading of boxes procedures.

components.name: Warehouse_box.cs
components.description: Control of the gravity of the boxes to be able to pick up them.

components.name: Robot_actuator.cs
components.description: Control of the pneumatic actuator of the robot.

components.name: Robot_arm.cs
components.description: Scalable script to control the joints of the robotic arm and its kinematics.

components.name: Robot_rightSensor.cs
components.description: Proximity sensor simulation capable of detecting objects in front and to the right of the robot.

metadata.title: Software architecture of the Unity simulation

components.name: Unity 3D Warehouse Environment
components.description: Contains Prefabs, Drop Zones, Warehouse Shelves, Robot Model.

components.name: Web Interface
components.description: Contains JavaScript Frontend, RecorderJS (Audio Recording).

components.name: CoppeliaSim
components.description: Contains Physics Engine, Robot Simulation.

components.name: Remote API
components.description: Contains sim.py (API Interface), simConst.py (Constants).

components.name: OCR Pipeline
components.description: Contains OCR_Lite.py, LettersNumbersClassification.py, LabelingRegions.py.

components.name: Python Scripts
components.description: Contains main.py (Entry Point), robot.py (Robot Class), cinematica_inversa.ipynb (Inverse Kinematics).

components.name: Cloud Services
components.description: Contains Google Cloud Functions, Speech-to-Text Service.

components.name: Data & Configuration
components.description: Contains IntelliJ IDEA Project Config, Git Repository.

comparison.explanation: The two diagrams represent different aspects of a software architecture related to a warehouse simulation. The GroundTruth diagram focuses on the Unity simulation components, detailing specific scripts and their roles in the simulation, such as robot control and warehouse management. The GeneratedImage diagram presents a broader architectural view, including layers like Presentation, Simulation, and Control & Logic, and emphasizes integration with cloud services and data configuration. There are no shared components or connectors between the diagrams, indicating they represent different levels or aspects of the system. The GroundTruth diagram is more detailed in terms of individual components, while the GeneratedImage diagram provides a higher-level overview with logical groupings. The GeneratedImage is rated 7 out of 10 for its accuracy and completeness, as it provides a comprehensive overview but lacks the specific details found in the GroundTruth diagram.
